criteria: The agent has read section 3.1 completely to understand the core methodology of Cross-Lingual Alignment.
The plan includes the preparation of source and target language sentences.
The plan includes the steps to calculate the optimal cross-lingual alignment matrix.
The code for the extraction of source and target sentences by taking the hidden state of the last token been implemented
The code has been implemented as equation (2) to derive the cross-lingual alignment matrix.
The agent has read section 3.2 completely to understand the core methodology of Inference-Time Transformation.
The plan includes the calculation of the intervention vector with the cross-lingual alignment matrix.
The plan includes the adjustment of blending with the hidden state in target language, controlled by ther hyperparameter $\alpha$
The code for the calculation of the intervention vector is implemented as equation (3).
The code for the adjustment of the intervention vector towards the target language is implemented as equation (4).
The agents have planned to implement the INCLINE method.
The main process of INCLINE method is coded by the agents.
The agent has read section 4.1 completely to check the experiment settings.
The datasets of discriminative tasks (XCOPA, XStoryCloze, XWinograd, XCSQA, XNLI) are planned to be collected and implemented.
The datasets of generative tasks (MZsRE, Flores, WMT23, MGSM) are planned to be collected and implemented.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: the INCLINE method consistently outperforms previous intervention methods (ITI, CAA) on discriminative tasks.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: the SFT + INCLINE method consistently outperforms the SFT baseline alone on discriminative tasks.

The agent has read Figure 3(a) to check the experiments about the training cost of INCLINE with regard to the number of parallel sentences.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3(a): the accuracy initially improves as the number of training sentences increases, but declines once the dataset becomes too large.

The agent has read Figure 4 to check the experiment settings of different $\alpha$ values.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: positive $\alpha$ values work better in Spanish and negative $\alpha$ values work better in Chinese.

The agent has read Figure 5(a) to check the In-Context Learning experiment settings.
